top_categories.workout_context: 186 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 186 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At Race Across Italy 300, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
81ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
186 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
15,092ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Race Across Italy 300's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 186 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Race Across Italy 300's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
15,092ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Race Across Italy 300. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 15,092ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Low-cadence force work for 15,092ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.heat: May in Silvi Paese, Teramo, Italy can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Race Across Italy 300. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 186 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 186 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Race Across Italy 300. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Race Across Italy 300 requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Endurance, and Over Under to prepare you for 186 miles of mixed terrain in Silvi Paese, Teramo, Italy.
